title: VayuDrishti Action Briefs
description: Verified, evidence-cited Action Briefs produced by the agentic layer (spec 14, owner: vayu-agents). Per-city file at web/public/data/{city}/briefs.json. Every brief is the output of Situation Analyst -> Causal Strategist -> Action Drafter -> Adversarial Verifier, then a DETERMINISTIC resolver gate. Contract discipline: numbers live ONLY in structured resolved fields (expected_effect, evidence_refs[].value); headline/situation/action are qualitative plain text. Every evidence_ref path resolves against a published artifact via the dotted-path resolver, and its value is the artifact's own ground-truth value (the pipeline overwrites the model's asserted value with the resolved one). Unresolvable or mismatched refs cause the brief to be repaired (<=2) then dropped, so only verified briefs are published (acceptance 18). All strings are HTML/control-char sanitized (spec 7); the publish gate greps for </think> and key material (acceptance 20).

generated_at.description: UTC ISO-8601 Z of the last SUCCESSFUL generation (spec 5.0). On a stale keep this stays at the last good run's time.

fixture.description: True when built from fixture/sample artifacts; propagated from the source artifacts' fixture flag. Consumers show a 'sample data' banner.

stale.description: True when the agent run failed and the previous briefs were kept (or an empty envelope emitted on cold start). Drives the web 'stale briefs' banner. Absent or false on a fresh successful run (spec 14 failure semantics, acceptance 19).

model.description: Model id used, e.g. nvidia/nemotron-3-ultra-550b-a55b. Disclosed for transparency (acceptance 20).

briefs.description: Ranked verified briefs (highest priority first). May be empty (no actionable compound risk, or a cold-start stale envelope).

brief_type.description: Brief category (spec 14/15). 'action' = compound-risk action brief (default). 'trigger-watch' = P(GRAP stage crossing) + ledger effect + pre-positioning window (spec 15.5). 'plume-alert' = FIRMS forward-advection ETA + affected wards (spec 15.2). 'data-quality' = anomalous station surfaced with evidence (spec 15.6). All share the same evidence_refs verifier discipline. Absent => 'action'.

headline.description: Qualitative one-line summary, plain text. Web MAY interpolate resolved values into a display string; the stored text carries no unverified numbers.

situation.description: Qualitative compound-risk narrative, plain text. Quantities belong in expected_effect / evidence_refs, not here.

action.description: The specific recommended action, plain text, rendered as-is.

action_code.description: Optional enforcement-taxonomy key (spec 5.4) for a localized label/icon. Display-only; the verifier NEVER keys off it. May be null when no single taxonomy action fits.

target_wards.description: ward_id list (convention spec 5.0); web joins to wards.geojson and highlights.

trigger_window.description: Action window in UTC (spec 5.0 storage); web displays IST with an 'IST' suffix. Content gate enforces start_utc <= end_utc.

ugm3.description: Expected PM2.5 effect in ug/m3 (sign per convention: negative = reduction). Resolved ground truth from the Intervention Ledger / interventions effects.

basis_ref.description: Dotted-path into ledger.json or interventions.json for the effect basis, e.g. ledger.wards[ward_id=delhi_042].effect_ugm3. Resolves via the resolver; external citations (CAQM orders, GEMM) are reachable through ledger.citations / receipts, not duplicated here.

expected_effect.description: Quantified expected effect with CI, or null when the brief carries no quantified effect (e.g. a pure public-health advisory). Content gate enforces ci_low <= ugm3 <= ci_high.

owner.description: Owner agency responsible for the action, plain text (e.g. MCD, DPCC, Delhi Traffic Police).

advisory_langs.description: ISO-639-1 codes for advisory dissemination (spec 5.5), e.g. [en, hi].

evidence_refs.description: Machine-resolvable citations. Every ref's path resolves against a published artifact; value is the artifact's ground-truth value (pipeline-overwritten). At least one ref per brief (acceptance 18).

label.description: Human label for the evidence chip, plain text (e.g. '24h forecast p90, ward delhi_042').

artifact.description: Published artifact this ref resolves into (per-city file, except receipts which is global).

path.description: Dotted-path with equality-predicate selectors, e.g. forecast.wards[ward_id=delhi_042].series[h=24].pm25_p90. Grammar: keys and [id_field=value] / [numeric_field=value] selectors and [index]; nothing else (LLM output is untrusted).

value.description: Resolved ground-truth value at path (pipeline overwrites the model's asserted value with the artifact's own).

resolved.description: Always true in published output; an unresolved ref causes the brief to be dropped.

ledger_ref.description: Optional linkage for the web /ledger slot: which Ledger stage_transition and/or counterfactual scenario this brief draws on.

passed.description: Adversarial Verifier verdict. A published brief always has passed=true AND cleared the deterministic resolver (the authoritative gate); the LLM verifier is advisory.

notes.description: Verifier notes, plain text (may be empty).
